source_jobs.title: Maintenance Technician - PACIFIKAVA 2nd Shift
source_jobs.description:
At Botanic Tonics, we believe being present is powerful, but in today's world it's very hard to do this a lot of the time. We exist to help people make the most of every moment—to reset, re-think, and then re-engage with themselves, others, and the world around them.

feel free products are thoughtfully crafted with noble kava root and other functional botanicals that have been used for centuries. Our feel free CLASSIC tonic and capsules contain noble kava root and natural kratom leaf, delivering calm, focused energy without caffeine. Our feel free Kava Maté combines noble kava root with yerba mate for elevated, sustained energy with 60mg of natural caffeine per serving. These unique blends are designed to create a state of chilled energy, helping to clear your mind and elevate your mood, so you can be more present and in the moment.

Founded in 2020, we now serve over 6 million servings monthly and are available in more than 27,000 retail locations nationwide. Our premium plant-based supplements are manufactured in an FDA-registered, cGMP-certified production facility in Broken Arrow, Oklahoma, where quality, science, and safety guide everything we do.



Overview

We are seeking a skilled and proactive Maintenance Technician to join our team on 2nd shift at our CO2 Extraction plant. The successful candidate will be responsible for maintaining and repairing equipment used in the extraction and grinding processes, as well as performing various facility maintenance tasks. This role is crucial to ensuring the smooth and efficient operation of our plant.



2nd Shift, 3:00 pm - 11:30 pm, Monday - Friday with flexible availability on Saturdays

*** Please note: Relocation assistance is available for candidates with experience in CO2 extraction process or other botanical extraction production ***

 

Key Responsibilities:

 - Perform routine maintenance and repairs on CO2 extraction and solids processing equipment

 - Troubleshoot and diagnose mechanical, electrical, and hydraulic issues

 - Conduct regular inspections of equipment to identify and address potential problems

 - Maintain accurate records of maintenance activities, including work orders and equipment logs

 - Assist in the installation and commissioning of new equipment

 - Perform miscellaneous facility maintenance tasks, such as plumbing, electrical work, and general repairs

 - Ensure compliance with safety regulations and company policies

 - Collaborate with the production team to minimize downtime and optimize equipment performance

 - Maintain an organized and clean work environment

 - Order and maintain inventory of spare parts and maintenance supplies

 - Participate in continuous improvement initiatives to enhance plant efficiency and reliability

 - Fabricate and weld as needed

 

Qualifications:

 - High school diploma or equivalent; technical certification or degree in a related field is preferred

 - 2+ years of experience in industrial maintenance, preferably in a manufacturing or extraction environment

 - Strong knowledge of mechanical, electrical, and hydraulic systems

 - Experience with CO2 extraction equipment is a plus

 - Ability to read and interpret technical manuals, schematics, and blueprints

 - Excellent problem-solving skills and attention to detail

 - Ability to work independently and as part of a team

 - Strong communication skills

 - Flexibility to work various shifts and respond to emergency maintenance calls as needed

 

Physical Requirements:

 - Ability to lift and move heavy equipment and materials (up to 50 lbs)

 - Ability to stand, walk, and bend for extended periods

 - Comfortable working in a manufacturing environment with exposure to various temperatures and noise levels

Benefits:

 - Health, dental, and vision insurance

 - Matching 401K

 - Parental leave

 - Paid time off

 - Opportunities for professional development and advancement

Relocation:

 - Relocation assistance is available for candidates with experien

source_jobs.title: Maintenance Technician
source_jobs.description:
At Botanic Tonics, we believe being present is powerful, but in today's world it's very hard to do this a lot of the time. We exist to help people make the most of every moment—to reset, re-think, and then re-engage with themselves, others, and the world around them.

feel free products are thoughtfully crafted with noble kava root and other functional botanicals that have been used for centuries. Our feel free CLASSIC tonic and capsules contain noble kava root and natural kratom leaf, delivering calm, focused energy without caffeine. Our feel free Kava Maté combines noble kava root with yerba mate for elevated, sustained energy with 60mg of natural caffeine per serving. These unique blends are designed to create a state of chilled energy, helping to clear your mind and elevate your mood, so you can be more present and in the moment.

Founded in 2020, we now serve over 6 million servings monthly and are available in more than 27,000 retail locations nationwide. Our premium plant-based supplements are manufactured in an FDA-registered, cGMP-certified production facility in Broken Arrow, Oklahoma, where quality, science, and safety guide everything we do.



Overview

The Maintenance Technician is responsible for performing routine maintenance, troubleshooting, and repair of all mechanical, electrical, and electronic systems in a manufacturing plant. This role ensures that all equipment is operating efficiently, safely, and in compliance with company standards and regulations. The technician will work closely with production staff to minimize downtime and maximize productivity.



Monday thru Thursday 2nd and 3rd shift needed (MUST be flexible to work other days as needed on occasion)



Key Responsibilities:

 - Routine Maintenance: Perform scheduled maintenance on plant equipment, including machinery, conveyors, and electrical systems

 - Troubleshooting and Repair: Diagnose and repair mechanical, electrical, and electronic issues in a timely manner to minimize production downtime

 - Installation: Assist with the installation of new equipment and systems, ensuring they meet safety and operational standards

 - Inspections: Conduct regular inspections of plant equipment to identify potential issues and perform preventive maintenance

 - Safety Compliance: Ensure all maintenance activities comply with safety regulations and company policies. Promote a safe working environment by adhering to all safety protocols

 - Documentation: Maintain accurate records of maintenance activities, including work orders, inspections, and repairs

 - Continuous Improvement: Suggest and implement improvements to enhance equipment reliability and efficiency

 - Emergency Response: Respond promptly to emergency breakdowns and work collaboratively with other team members to resolve issues

 - Performs other duties as assigned



Qualifications:

 - High school diploma or equivalent required

 - 2+ years of maintenance experience within a manufacturing environment

 - Strong knowledge of mechanical, electrical, and electronic systems. Proficiency in using diagnostic tools and equipment

 - Excellent troubleshooting skills with the ability to diagnose and repair complex issues

 - Good verbal and written communication skills. Ability to read and interpret technical manuals and schematics

 - Ability to work effectively in a team-oriented environment and collaborate with production staff and other technicians



Physical Requirements:

 - Must be able to lift up to 50 pounds

 - Must be able to bend, stoop, kneel, reach and climb to perform duties




BENEFITS OVERVIEW: COMPREHENSIVE MEDICAL, DENTAL AND VISION PLANS, 401K MATCH, FAMILY LEAVE, PTO & PAID HOLIDAY SCHEDULE.




BOTANIC TONICS IS AN EQUAL EMPLOYMENT OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER. CANDIDATES ARE CONSIDERED FOR EMPLOYMENT WITHOUT REGARD TO RACE, CREED, COLOR, NATIONAL ORIGIN, AGE, SEX, RELIGION, ANCESTRY, VETERAN STATUS, MARITAL STATUS, GENDER IDENTIFY, SEXUAL ORIENTATION, NATIONAL ORIGIN, LIABILITY

source_jobs.title: Head of International Sales
source_jobs.description:
At Botanic Tonics, we believe being present is powerful, but in today's world it's very hard to do this a lot of the time. We exist to help people make the most of every moment—to reset, re-think, and then re-engage with themselves, others, and the world around them.

feel free products are thoughtfully crafted with noble kava root and other functional botanicals that have been used for centuries. Our feel free CLASSIC tonic and capsules contain noble kava root and natural kratom leaf, delivering calm, focused energy without caffeine. Our feel free Kava Maté combines noble kava root with yerba mate for elevated, sustained energy with 60mg of natural caffeine per serving. These unique blends are designed to create a state of chilled energy, helping to clear your mind and elevate your mood, so you can be more present and in the moment.

Founded in 2020, we now serve over 6 million servings monthly and are available in more than 27,000 retail locations nationwide. Our premium plant-based supplements are manufactured in an FDA-registered, cGMP-certified production facility in Broken Arrow, Oklahoma, where quality, science, and safety guide everything we do.



Overview

As our first Head of International Sales, this is a rare opportunity to join the ground floor of a high-growth CPG brand and shape how we go to market across multiple geographies simultaneously. You will work directly with the parent company to set up distribution and retail partnerships and build the team and infrastructure needed to scale internationally.

 

Key Responsibilities:

Go-to-Market Strategy

 - Define and execute the international sales strategy across EMEA, APAC, Latin America, and other target regions

 - Identify and prioritize markets based on demand signals, competitive landscape, and distribution potential

Revenue & Distribution

 - Build and manage a pipeline of retail, grocery, e-commerce, and distributor partnerships globally

 - Negotiate and close distribution agreements, ranging from regional distributors to national retail chains

 - Own international revenue targets and report directly to the Chief Sales Officer

Partnerships & Relationship Management

 - Cultivate long-term relationships with key buyers, brokers, and distributors across markets

 - Represent the brand at international trade shows, industry events, and buyer meetings

 - Partner with marketing to ensure brand consistency and localization across regions

Team & Operations

 - Hire, develop, and lead regional sales managers and account executives as the team scales



Qualifications:

Required

 - 10-15+ years of international sales experience, ideally within CPG, FMCG, or consumer brands

 - Proven track record of building distribution partnerships and closing deals across multiple international markets

 - Experience working in or closely with startups or high-growth companies where you've had to build from scratch

 - Strong commercial acumen with the ability to balance short-term revenue goals with long-term market development

 - Excellent relationship-building and negotiation skills across cultures and business environments

 - Willingness to travel abroad

Nice to Have

 - Experience scaling a CPG brand from seed stage into international distribution

 - Existing relationships with distributors and/or major retailers across EMEA, APAC, or Latin America

 - Fluency in a second language (Spanish, French, Mandarin, or Arabic a plus)

 - Familiarity with export logistics, trade compliance, and international pricing strategies



What we offer:

 - Competitive base salary commensurate with experience

 - Performance-based bonus tied to company milestones

 - Flexible remote-first work environment with travel budget

 - A chance to build something from scratch and leave a lasting mark on a global brand
   
   

Our Values:

 - Builder mindset: We thrive in ambiguity and take ownership of outcomes

 - Global curiosity: We respect and adapt to the nuances of every market

source_jobs.title: Sales Recruiter
source_jobs.description:
At Botanic Tonics, we believe being present is powerful, but in today's world it's very hard to do this a lot of the time. We exist to help people make the most of every moment—to reset, re-think, and then re-engage with themselves, others, and the world around them.

feel free products are thoughtfully crafted with noble kava root and other functional botanicals that have been used for centuries. Our feel free CLASSIC tonic and capsules contain noble kava root and natural kratom leaf, delivering calm, focused energy without caffeine. Our feel free Kava Maté combines noble kava root with yerba mate for elevated, sustained energy with 60mg of natural caffeine per serving. These unique blends are designed to create a state of chilled energy, helping to clear your mind and elevate your mood, so you can be more present and in the moment.

Founded in 2020, we now serve over 6 million servings monthly and are available in more than 27,000 retail locations nationwide. Our premium plant-based supplements are manufactured in an FDA-registered, cGMP-certified production facility in Broken Arrow, Oklahoma, where quality, science, and safety guide everything we do.



Overview

We are seeking a highly skilled Recruiter with 3–5 years of demonstrated success in high-volume recruiting, preferably within the Consumer Packaged Goods (CPG) industry or related fields. The ideal candidate has substantial experience sourcing and hiring for sales roles such as field sales representatives, account managers, or other client-facing positions that drive business growth. This role requires a driven individual who thrives in fast-paced environments and can effectively manage multiple requisitions while maintaining a stellar candidate experience. 



Responsibilities:

 - End-to-End Recruitment: Support the full recruitment cycle for high-volume roles, particularly sales positions, including sourcing, screening, interviewing, and onboarding candidates

 - Strategic Sourcing: Utilize innovative sourcing techniques, including job boards, social media platforms, and referrals, to identify top talent

 - Industry Expertise Leverage your understanding of the CPG industry to attract candidates who align with our organizational goals

 - Candidate Experience: Build strong relationships with candidates, ensuring a seamless and positive recruitment journey

 - Stakeholder Collaboration: Partner with internal stakeholders to understand their needs and align recruitment strategies to meet workforce goals

 - Data-Driven Recruitment: Maintain accurate records of recruitment metrics to assess pipeline health and drive continuous improvement

 

Qualifications:

Must haves

 - Bachelor's degree in Business, Marketing, or a related field

 - 3-5+ years experience in high-volume recruiting, preferably within the Consumer Packaged Goods (CPG) industry or similar

 - Proven track record of success recruiting for sales roles or comparable positions

 - Strong ability to prioritize, multitask, and manage multiple requisitions under tight deadlines

 - Excellent communication and relationship-building abilities, with a focus on delivering a great candidate experience.

 - Experience with Applicant Tracking System (ATS) and tools like LinkedIn Recruiter, Indeed, ZipRecruiter, and other sourcing platforms

 - Ability to analyze data, draw insights, and make strategic recommendations

Nice to haves

 - Background in recruitment for field sales, merchandising, or territory management roles

 - Familiarity with employer branding strategies and employee value propositions

 - Knowledge of compliance and best recruitment practices
   
   

 


BENEFITS OVERVIEW: COMPREHENSIVE MEDICAL, DENTAL AND VISION PLANS, 401K MATCH, FAMILY LEAVE, PTO & PAID HOLIDAY SCHEDULE.




BOTANIC TONICS IS AN EQUAL EMPLOYMENT OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER. CANDIDATES ARE CONSIDERED FOR EMPLOYMENT WITHOUT REGARD TO RACE, CREED, COLOR, NATIONAL ORIGIN, AGE, SEX, RELIGION, ANCESTRY, VETERAN STATUS, MARITAL STATUS,

source_jobs.title: Director of National Accounts
source_jobs.description:
At Botanic Tonics, we believe being present is powerful, but in today's world it's very hard to do this a lot of the time. We exist to help people make the most of every moment—to reset, re-think, and then re-engage with themselves, others, and the world around them.

feel free products are thoughtfully crafted with noble kava root and other functional botanicals that have been used for centuries. Our feel free CLASSIC tonic and capsules contain noble kava root and natural kratom leaf, delivering calm, focused energy without caffeine. Our feel free Kava Maté combines noble kava root with yerba mate for elevated, sustained energy with 60mg of natural caffeine per serving. These unique blends are designed to create a state of chilled energy, helping to clear your mind and elevate your mood, so you can be more present and in the moment.

Founded in 2020, we now serve over 6 million servings monthly and are available in more than 27,000 retail locations nationwide. Our premium plant-based supplements are manufactured in an FDA-registered, cGMP-certified production facility in Broken Arrow, Oklahoma, where quality, science, and safety guide everything we do.



OVERVIEW



As the Director of National Accounts at Botanic Tonics, you will play a critical role in driving growth and maximizing sales opportunities within the national convenience store channel. This position requires a strategic thinker with a strong understanding of the convenience store market landscape and a proven track record of developing and executing successful sales strategies. You will be responsible for building and maintaining relationships with key accounts, driving product distribution, and implementing trade programs to increase brand visibility and drive sales.



TASKS, DUTIES, & GENERAL RESPONSIBILITIES



Strategic Account Management and BD:

 - Aggressively pursue new business opportunities within the chain store segment, identifying potential clients and executing strategies to secure contracts

 - Collaborate with the marketing team to develop targeted sales and promotional strategies that align with the unique selling points of BT

 - Build and maintain strong, long-lasting relationships with key accounts in the national chain store segment

 - Develop a deep understanding of each key account's business and leverage this insight to create tailored strategies that drive sales and partnership growth

Contract Negotiation and Management:

 - Negotiate and manage contracts with key accounts, ensuring terms are favorable and in line with company goals and standards

 - Facile with financials and can quickly determine how to position/negotiate deals to BT’s advantage

Market Analysis and Adaptation:

 - Continuously monitor market trends, competitor activities, and consumer preferences within BT’s product sector

 - Adapt sales strategies in response to market shifts and emerging opportunities to maintain and grow market share

Product Representation and Advocacy:

 - Serve as the primary representative and advocate for our products in all interactions with key accounts

 - Ensure product positioning and branding are consistently conveyed and aligned with our company values and standards

Performance Tracking and Reporting:

 - Set, monitor, and review performance metrics for key accounts, using data-driven insights to guide strategy and decision-making

 - Regularly report to senior management with account performance analyses, market insights, and strategic recommendations

Team Collaboration and Leadership:

 - Work closely with cross-functional teams, including marketing, DSDs, and supply chain, to ensure seamless execution of account strategies

 - Provide thought leadership and market insights to support company-wide strategy. Potentially grow a team over time, including hiring, developing, and ensuring high performance
   
   

QUALIFICATIONS



Education, Technical Skills, and/or Previous Experience:

 - Bachelor's degree in Business, Sales, Mark

source_jobs.title: Production Technician - PACIFIKAVA, 2nd Shift
source_jobs.description:
At Botanic Tonics, we believe being present is powerful, but in today's world it's very hard to do this a lot of the time. We exist to help people make the most of every moment—to reset, re-think, and then re-engage with themselves, others, and the world around them.

feel free products are thoughtfully crafted with noble kava root and other functional botanicals that have been used for centuries. Our feel free CLASSIC tonic and capsules contain noble kava root and natural kratom leaf, delivering calm, focused energy without caffeine. Our feel free Kava Maté combines noble kava root with yerba mate for elevated, sustained energy with 60mg of natural caffeine per serving. These unique blends are designed to create a state of chilled energy, helping to clear your mind and elevate your mood, so you can be more present and in the moment.

Founded in 2020, we now serve over 6 million servings monthly and are available in more than 27,000 retail locations nationwide. Our premium plant-based supplements are manufactured in an FDA-registered, cGMP-certified production facility in Broken Arrow, Oklahoma, where quality, science, and safety guide everything we do.



Overview

We are hiring a Production Technician, 2nd shift who is responsible for operating and maintaining solids processing for CO₂ extraction equipment to support the production of botanical-based products. This role ensures processes are executed safely, efficiently, and in compliance with quality and regulatory standards.

This is a hands-on role requiring strong attention to detail, technical aptitude, and the ability to troubleshoot and support production operations in a manufacturing environment.



We are hiring for 2nd shift, M - F, 3:00PM - 11:30PM with flexibility to work some Saturdays



Responsibilities:

Production Operations

· Operate and monitor solids processing for CO₂ extraction equipment

· Prepare and process raw materials to meet production specifications

· Ensure production processes follow established procedures and schedules



Equipment & Troubleshooting

· Monitor and interact with automated equipment control systems during production operations

· Perform routine equipment inspection

· Assist with equipment setup, calibration, and cleaning prior to production runs



Quality & Process Monitoring

· Monitor production processes to ensure consistent product quality

· Collect samples and record data

· Support quality standards and product specifications in coordination with QC and Lab teams



Documentation & Inventory

· Maintain accurate production logs, work orders, and operational data

· Assist with handling of raw materials and inventory



Safety & Compliance

· Follow all safety protocols, including handling of CO₂ and other materials

· Comply with cGMP and company standards

· Maintain a clean, organized, and safe work environment



Continuous Improvement

· Identify process improvements to increase efficiency, yield, and product quality

· Support team efforts to improve equipment reliability and reduce waste



Qualifications:

· High school diploma or equivalent required

· Food industry experience preferred

· 1–2+ years of production or manufacturing experience preferred

· Experience operating PLC-controlled or automated processing equipment preferred

· Basic computer skills and ability to enter, review, and maintain production records electronically

· Mechanical aptitude and ability to use hand tools and measurement devices

· Strong attention to detail and communication skills



Physical Requirements

· Ability to lift up to 57 pounds

· Ability to stand, walk, climb, bend, and reach regularly

· Comfortable working around machinery and in varying conditions

· Ability to wear required PPE and work in a manufacturing environment



Please Note: This position is with PACIFIKAVA LLC, a wholly owned subsidiary of Botanic Tonics, LLC.




BENEFITS OVERVIEW: COMPREHENSIVE MEDICAL, DENTAL AND VISION PLANS, 401K MATCH, FAMILY LE

notes: Botanic Tonics is a consumer goods company with a focus on botanical-based products. The job postings suggest a strong emphasis on sales and production, with no explicit tech roles.
